name: workflow
description: BeSpunky ways-of-working: the process, order, and methodology of how work moves from idea to production — independent of what's being built. branch-and-release: the house git methodology — a one-way promotion pipeline across four branches (feature → development → staging → main, each a strict ancestor of the next so promotions are clean fast-forwards), unrelated work isolated in per-feature git worktrees off the integration branch (with the Nx-workspace-root override that stops tooling silently targeting the main tree), the relevance check that runs before touching any file, rebase-and-re-verify at the one divergence point (feature → development) rather than resolving conflicts on the shared branch, and three human-gated promotions (feature done → development; go to staging; go live → main). Deploy bindings (which branches auto-deploy to which environment) are a per-project parameter stated in the project's CLAUDE.md; this skill carries the durable method. Serving an in-flight worktree without merging it back is provided by the unified serve target in BeSpunky-scaffolded (Nx) projects (`nx serve <app> --worktree=<branch|slug>`, with automatic per-worktree port isolation and a pretty <slug>.localhost domain). local-server-isolation: when launching a local server to test a change, bind a RANDOM free port (never the default/forwarded port, which belongs to a server the user launched manually) — you test headless so you never need the forwarded ports; and never reap the user's running Firebase emulator suite (reuse it, or run your own on a shifted port set).
